title: Market Brief — Saturday, March 28, 2026
summary: B200 spot surged 17.6% overnight to $9.19/hr, the sharpest single-day GPU move in the dataset, while availability sits at just 29%. This signals a demand squeeze on next-gen silicon that will pressure inference margins for anyone not locked into long-term contracts.
paragraphs: B200 spot surged 17.6% overnight to $9.19/hr, the sharpest single-day GPU move in the dataset, while availability sits at just 29%. This signals a demand squeeze on next-gen silicon that will pressure inference margins for anyone not locked into long-term contracts.

DeepSeek V3.2 sells output at $0.38/M against a TCO-adjusted Llama 70B production cost north of $5/M on H100s — they are burning cash at industrial scale, subsidizing adoption by selling at a loss of roughly $4.30 per million tokens. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at 90%+ margins, while B200 operators running medium-tier pricing are underwater at negative 124% margins — every token served destroys value.

B200 spot at $11.49 TCO-adjusted is overvalued relative to H200 at $2.90, which delivers comparable Llama 70B throughput at 40% of the effective cost. Rotate GPU exposure from B200 to H200 spot and harvest the spread before availability tightens further.
bullets: 21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch three years ago.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is the cheapest token on the market, priced 99.5% below o3 Pro.
29% B200 availability is the tightest supply among all three GPU classes tracked.
$2.32/hr H200 spot dropped 5.2% day-over-day, the only GPU class to cheapen.
97% ultra-tier margin on H100 and H200 confirms premium API pricing remains the only reliably profitable inference play.
$0.60/M output from Llama 4 Maverick undercuts every closed-source frontier model by at least 4x.
